behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is the handoff between manifest/lockfile parsing, transitive resolution, and the vulnerability-check input assembly. A unit test of only the resolver can miss dropped dependencies at module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture manifests + lockfiles with known direct/transitive trees, run the real extraction pipeline, and assert the vulnerability-check request set contains every expected package+version pair (no omissions/duplicates).

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real HTTP/API contract semantics for version-range query parameters and response filtering. Mocking can hide real failures in encoding, query shape, or server-side interpretation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a contract test against a real vuln API endpoint (or official sandbox) using the same package across multiple version ranges, and verify returned CVEs match only the manifest’s exact range.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within a local function/object boundary, with no required I/O or cross-module contract to expose the target failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list spanning severities and mixed CVSS values; assert final order is severity rank first, then CVSS descending within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: The failure usually appears at parser/editor/serializer boundaries (and file round-trip behavior), not just in isolated version-selection logic.
behaviors.test_description: Apply the real manifest updater to temp-file fixtures and assert diff scope is only the targeted dependency version; verify all other dependency entries remain identical.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires real repository/remote PR behavior (git + hosting API). Local mocks can pass while failing on auth, branch/ref handling, or server-side diff behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against a real test repository: create branch, commit patched manifest, open PR via real API, then assert PR exists and changed files/diff show exactly one dependency bump.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local comparison logic; no external system or multi-module wiring is required to surface the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver pairs (including edge cases like pre-release/build metadata) and assert `breaking=true` only when effective major version increases.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans decision logic, PR-creation module invocation, and output/report assembly. The failure is at local module interactions, not external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner orchestration with real semver decision + output formatter and a spy/fake PR client; feed a breaking bump and assert PR path is not invoked and review-required flag appears in emitted results.
